TextENGNew: ...Suddenly, my vision opened up, and a cold wind swept across my entire body.
...It was... a hilltop overlooking the village...
Looking down from this hilltop, I could see lights flickering on throughout the village... It was clear that the whole community was being roused from its slumber.
...First Takano-san and the others, then the village chief, and now... even Rika-chan and Satoko had become victims... I could see the news rippling through the village...
changeReason: Japanese uses 'たち' (and others) after Takano-san; original adds Tomitake-san specifically, while new uses 'and the others' matching the Japanese grouping.

TextENGNew: My knees buckled beneath me.@k ......This was... no longer something that could be laughed off.

The two of them would have been found safe and sound. Mion would have teased me for being such a worrywart, and Rena would have smiled wryly, saying, 'But really, isn't it great that they're okay!'@k ...That was how it should have gone...
...The outcome I had wanted most faded away like a cooling ember.@k ...The scene before me made that painfully clear.
...Not just my knees—I doubled over, pressing both palms flat against the ground... and dug my nails into the earth.
......I couldn't tell whether what I felt was sorrow or regret......
...Because I... couldn't bear the weight of my own sin... and had gone and told Rika-chan everything......!
Rika-chan and... Satoko too... they had nothing to do with any of it, and yet they'd been... sacrificed...!!
...This was a sin that Shion and I each had to carry in silence... shouldering it until the day we ourselves were erased.
......No... if I traced it back far enough, the problem started much, much earlier than that.@k ...The original sin was trespassing into the ritual storehouse... and breaking the taboo.
I knew from the very beginning that it was a place I should never have entered!
......And yet... I gave in to cheap curiosity......
Never in my life had I hated myself as much as I did at this very moment......
changeReason: Japanese '悔しい' expresses frustration/vexation/regret; original uses 'angry at myself' while new escalates to 'hated myself,' which shifts the emotional register.

TextENGNew: There was a crunching of gravel.
...Sunk as deep in despair as I was, I had no interest in whoever was approaching.
``......Keiichi-kun?@k@vS02/02/130200236. ......Keiichi-kun!@k@vS02/02/130200237. Are you all right?!``
It was Rena.
...The way I was clawing at the ground must have made it look like I was doubled over in pain.
...She hurried over and gently rubbed my back, trying to comfort me.
``......It's... all my fault. Everything.@k@vS02/01/130100703. ......Because of me...@k@vS02/01/130100704. ...It's all because of me...``
``...Keiichi-kun's fault?@k@vS02/02/130200239. You haven't done anything wrong.@k@vS02/02/130200240. Please... don't blame yourself...``
I could tell that Rena was choosing her words with the utmost care, out of concern for me.
...And precisely because of that, I could sense what lay behind those words.
Keiichi-kun, you know why Rika-chan and Satoko-chan disappeared, don't you?@k ......That was what she was really asking.
......Because it had been an emergency, Rena had let slide the question of why I had suddenly become desperate to confirm Rika-chan's safety, but...
......Now that Rika-chan's disappearance had actually been confirmed, it was only natural that Rena would want to know the reason.
......But... things had gone this far now... I couldn't possibly tell even Rena...
I didn't want to accept that Rika-chan and Satoko were gone... but even less could I allow Rena to disappear as well.
``......Keiichi-kun.@k@vS02/02/130200242. You'll catch a cold if you stay out in this wind...``
Rena sat down on the ground, lowering herself to my eye level.
``...They told us kids to go home and get some sleep.``
She said this while showing me her wristwatch.@k ...It was already well past one in the morning.
...Time was passing far too quickly...
......Had I really been sprawled out here for that long...?

``We need to sleep, or... tomorrow's classes will be rough.@k@vS02/02/130200245. Even the grown-ups are exhausted—they were up all last night searching for the village chief too.@k@vS02/02/130200246. ......They said they'd call it off a bit earlier tonight.``
......I felt a flicker of anger that they weren't devoting as much time to this search as they had for the village chief... but I, the root cause of everything, had no right to criticize anyone...
``It'll be okay.@k@vS02/02/130200248. The police arrived a little while ago.@k@vS02/02/130200249. Look—more patrol cars keep coming in from town right now.``
When she said that, I looked down at the village and saw that, sure enough, an unusual number of cars for this hour were streaming in from the darkness beyond the mountains.
...But none of them had their emergency lights on.@k And of course, none of them were running their sirens either.
......That's right.
......Every incident believed to be Oyashiro-sama's curse, occurring right after Watanagashi... was always dealt with in secrecy.
Rika-chan and Satoko, too... just like the serial incidents of years past—@kquietly,@k left unsolved,@k shrouded in mystery... it would all just end... and fade away.
Rika-chan's face... that adorable face, like a porcelain doll's... was fading away.
...Satoko's smile... that spirited grin with her little fangs peeking out... was fading away.

......The harder I tried to remember them, the blurrier they became......@k I could have been called a fool seven times over and it still wouldn't have been enough—I couldn't hold back the tears at my own wretched stupidity........
``......I don't know what you're blaming yourself for, Keiichi-kun... but please, stop?``
I couldn't deny it.
But I couldn't confirm it either.
......At the very least, I didn't want to drag Rena into this.
......If she learns the truth... she'll be dragged in too...
``......Please, Rena.@k@vS02/01/130100707. ......Could you just... leave me be?@k@vS02/01/130100708. ......Even if I go home... there's no way I'll be able to sleep.``
...If I wasn't going to sleep anyway... then I at least wanted to keep searching for Rika-chan and the others until I collapsed.
changeReason: Japanese '梨花ちゃんたち' means 'Rika-chan and the others'; original specifies Satoko by name while new uses 'and the others,' matching the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ......The entire village had turned out to search and still hadn't found them.
...At this point, even if I searched on my own... I couldn't imagine... I'd find anything.
...But still... I had to keep looking......
``......They disappeared...... because of me.@k@vS02/01/130100710. ......How could I possibly... go to sleep... before all the others who are out there searching...``
I had meant it as bitter self-reproach, muttered to myself... but I quickly realized that Rena was silently trying to parse the meaning behind my words.
......I realized I'd said too much.
``......It's Keiichi-kun's fault?@k@vS02/02/130200252. Why would you think that? Why?``
That can't be true.
It's not your fault, Keiichi-kun.
...I was grateful that Rena said those things... but she only said them because she didn't know the truth.
...If she knew that everything had been set in motion by the taboo I broke on the night of Watanagashi, even Rena would curse me...
...But I couldn't even allow myself to be cursed by her.
...Because that would mean confessing my sin to Rena.
If I let slip even a single word about sneaking into the ritual storehouse on Watanagashi night... then even Rena... might vanish by the next nightfall.
......This gentle, devoted, always playful, always hau-ing... and yet, in moments like these, the most dependable person I could ask for... Rena might... vanish.
I couldn't bear that.@k ...Absolutely not!
This time... I had to carry this sin alone.@k By myself.

``............I know how much you care about your friends, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS02/02/130200254. I understand the urge to punish yourself.@k@vS02/02/130200255. ...But Rena knows it's not Keiichi-kun's fault.@k@vS02/02/130200256. I know you didn't do anything wrong, Keiichi-kun.``
``Y-You... you know that...?!``
A chill ran through my veins... crawling up my spine... and turned the sorrow in my heart to sheer dread.
I... I didn't tell her anything, did I?!
Had I... had I let something slip that tipped Rena off?!
This is bad... this is bad...!!
At this rate... Rena will vanish, Rena will vanish, Rena will vanish...!!
...Then Rena, just the way I always did to the others... grabbed my head and ruffled my hair.
``......Keiichi-kun.@k@vS02/02/130200258. Are you afraid... that Rena might vanish too?@k@vS02/02/130200259. .........Because of Oyashiro-sama's curse?``
She kept stroking my head, even more firmly now.
``Then put your mind at ease.@k@vS02/02/130200261. Rena isn't going to vanish.@k@vS02/02/130200262. I promise.``
``...H-How... how can you say that... with such certainty...?!``
Someone has been sacrificed every single night!
What guarantee was there that it wouldn't happen again tomorrow night?!
...And there was even less guarantee... that Rena wouldn't be the next sacrifice...!
......I don't care who you are.
...Please, if you're going to erase someone... just erase me first...!
Stop this cruelty of taking the people closest to us—the village chief for Shion, Rika-chan and Satoko for me—please, just stop...!!
...Shion's hysterical scream echoed in my mind...
They're planning to kill us last of all.

They won't kill us outright—instead they'll kill those closest to us, one by one... and only after they've made us suffer every possible grief will they finally kill us.@k That must be what they're after.
Please... don't disappear...@k ...Until I'm gone... please... please don't disappear......
I could no longer hold back the sobs rising within me.

``......Come on, tears don't suit you, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS02/02/130200264. Let's go back to the others.@k@vS02/02/130200265. ...The ladies' association is serving miso soup.@k@vS02/02/130200266. A warm bowl will calm you right down.``
Rena urged me to stand.
...I didn't have the will to resist, so I rose as she guided me.
When we returned to the shrine grounds, several portable gas stoves had been set up with large pots of miso soup simmering on them.
A great number of villagers sat in stony silence, sipping the soup that had been served to them, as if the life had been drained out of them.
Their faces were utterly exhausted...
Every one of them must have been combing through the village until just moments ago.
...But from their somber expressions, I could tell they hadn't turned up a single lead...
``...Kei-chan, where'd you run off to?@k@vS02/03/130300217. I was worried you'd gone missing too!``
Mion offered me some miso soup, but I had no appetite and turned it down.
``...Mii-chan.@k@vS02/02/130200268. Any luck?@k@vS02/02/130200269. ...Did you find any clues?``
Mion took a sip of her soup... her expression hollow.

``......Nothing at all.@k@vS02/03/130300219. After school, they went home.@k@vS02/03/130300220. Then the two of them apparently rode off somewhere on their bikes.@k@vS02/03/130300221. ...And on top of that, nobody saw them cycling anywhere...``
Once it gets dark in Hinamizawa, people vanish from the streets almost instantly.
...Apart from adults hurrying home from town by car or motorcycle, it was perfectly normal to see not a single soul walking outside.
...Even if nobody had seen Rika-chan and Satoko on their bicycles, that wasn't the least bit strange.
``......Same as last night with the village chief.@k@vS02/03/130300223. ......We've hit a dead end.``
changeReason: Japanese '村長' means 'village chief/head'; new correctly uses 'village chief' while original uses 'mayor,' which implies a different type of governance.

TextENGNew: Mion's detached tone grated on my nerves...... but the anger subsided almost immediately.
She had been doing everything in her power to search for Rika-chan until just moments ago.
...From what I'd heard, Mion had stayed up the entire previous night searching for the village chief as well.
...She must have been far more exhausted than I was.
......And what about me?
......I had just been crouching by myself, wallowing in misery.@k ...I had no right to say a word about Mion.

``...I know it's hard, Kei-chan, but let's rest for tonight.@k@vS02/03/130300225. ...They just decided to call off the search for now.@k@vS02/03/130300226. Finish your soup and let's call it a night.``
``............Is this... really the end...?``

``...Oh, no, it's not over.@k@vS02/03/130300228. Once the sun's up, the police are bringing in reinforcements for a thorough search.@k@vS02/03/130300229. They might have gone to Okinomiya, after all.@k@vS02/03/130300230. They're saying they'll canvas all of Shishibone City for any sightings—the village chief's case included.``

``More importantly, it's you I'm worried about, Kei-chan!@k@vS02/03/130300232. Where on earth did you go?!@k@vS02/03/130300233. ...You just vanished without a word... I was really worried, you know?!``
Suddenly the spotlight was on me, but I had no energy to push back.
``.........Sorry... I'll be more careful...``
``Nothing happened to you, right?@k@vS02/03/130300235. Nobody suspicious was watching or following you... nothing like that...?!``
Mion pressed me with a serious look in her eyes.
...It felt like I was being scolded for being so careless.
......I was hurting this much, and yet—@k being chided for carelessness stung... but this was my sin to bear.
...My sin, and mine alone to carry.
``......Nothing happened.@k@vS02/01/130100719. ...But I'll be careful...@k@vS02/01/130100720. ......Sorry.``
Once Mion heard that nothing had happened, she let out a deep sigh of relief, her shoulders visibly relaxing.
``...Oh, my, my, my...!@k@vS02/11/130700091. I thought I caught a whiff of something delicious.@k@vS02/11/130700092. I'd be most grateful if you'd let me have some.``
A grating, oily voice cut through the air—deep and unmistakably male.
It was Ooishi-san, trailed by several police officers.
...He was putting on an energetic front that felt jarringly out of place here.
``......There's hardly any left.@k@vS02/03/130300237. But help yourself if you want some.``
...Mion and Ooishi-san had never gotten along.
Her cold demeanor, however, didn't faze Ooishi-san in the slightest.@k It was like water off a frog's back.

``Well, would you look at that...@k@vS02/11/130700094. The dregs of miso soup are always a bit too salty, you know.@k@vS02/11/130700095. ...Oh well, na-ha-ha-ha...!@k@vS02/11/130700096. Any takers among you folks?``
He offered it to his subordinates, but they all declined with awkward smiles.
``...So, have the police turned up any clues?``
In place of miso soup, Rena welcomed him with a warm smile.
``Don't you worry, young lady.@k@vS02/11/130700098. Rest assured, rest assured!@k@vS02/11/130700099. We're searching quite thoroughly, I assure you.@k@vS02/11/130700100. Nfu-fu-fu-fu!``
He hadn't said a single word about finding any new clues.
In other words, there had been no progress.@k ...What a slimy, roundabout way of dodging the question.
``In any case.@k@vS02/11/130700102. It really is quite late.@k@vS02/11/130700103. ...All you young people should head on home and get some rest.@k@vS02/11/130700104. It's not good for your health.``
``......He's right.@k@vS02/03/130300239. Kei-chan, Rena—get some rest tonight.@k@vS02/03/130300240. Leave the rest to these guys—that's what our tax money pays for.``
``We pay taxes too, you know.@k@vS02/11/130700106. ...I feel like a soba chef eating his own noodles. Na-ha-ha-ha-ha...!``
...I was in no mood to laugh.@k Ooishi-san was the only one cackling like an idiot.
``Then... we're counting on you, truly.@k@vS02/02/130200272. ...Let's head home, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS02/02/130200273. Tomorrow will be rough if we don't.``
``Absolutely right.@k@vS02/11/130700108. I can give you two a ride, if you'd like.@k@vS02/11/130700109. Kuma-chan, would you bring the car around?``
``Oh... no, that's all right.@k@vS02/01/130100722. ...We brought our bicycles, so...``
``Not to worry.@k@vS02/11/130700111. We came in a wagon.@k@vS02/11/130700112. We can load the bikes right in.``
Sleepy as I was, it was just a bike ride home.@k ...I didn't want to be in the police's debt.
...Rena, however, nodded.
``Since they're offering, let's take them up on it.@k@vS02/02/130200275. ...It's probably safer than going back on our own.``
If safety was the concern, Rena's thinking was perfectly rational.
``Then it's settled—let's be off, shall we?``
``See you, then.@k@vS02/03/130300242. Good night...@k@vS02/03/130300243. both of you—Rena, Kei-chan.``
``Mm.@k@vS02/02/130200277. Get some rest soon too, Mii-chan, okay?@k@vS02/02/130200278. ......Good night.``
I waved as well, exchanging a silent farewell...
......Rika-chan and Satoko... were still nowhere to be found.
...But that gnawing anxiety... was gradually being swept away by the rising tide of exhaustion.
When Rena pointed out where our bicycles were, Ooishi-san hoisted them effortlessly into the police wagon.
We were ushered into the back seat.
The springs squeaked, and the seat was the kind that would normally make your rear end ache... but just then, it felt impossibly soft—more than comfortable enough to lull me to sleep.
``Ladies first, then.@k@vS02/11/130700115. We'll drop off the young lady first.@k@vS02/11/130700116. What's your address?``
``Oh, th-thank you.@k@vS02/02/130200280. My house is, um, let's see......``
......Rena's voice slowly grew distant.
...I felt myself being pulled into the depths of sleep.
``......Helloooo?@k@vS02/11/130700118. ...Maebara-saaaan... have you completely fallen asleep on us?``
......When I came to, everything was quiet.@k The car was stopped.
Ooishi-san was lightly tapping my cheek.
...It wasn't pleasant in the least, so I snapped awake right away.
``...Ah...... ngh... sorry... I completely dozed off......``
Something was thrust right under my nose.
...A lump of metal?@k ......It was a can of coffee.
``It's gone lukewarm, but@k@vS02/11/130700120. sweet cafe au lait goes down just fine at room temperature.``
I took the can mechanically and popped the pull-tab.
......The lukewarm, sweet coffee slid down my throat... gradually rousing my sleep-fogged mind...
...As the drowsiness lifted... I began to wonder why the car had stopped, and why I was being offered coffee.
Ooishi-san, who should have been in the driver's seat, had at some point moved to the back seat beside me.
......All at once, a very bad feeling crept over me.
It reminded me of the grilling he'd given me at the library the other day.
......By the time I realized my mistake, it was already too late.
...Today, of all days, there would be no escaping.
...He clearly had no intention of letting me go until he heard answers that satisfied him...
``Na-ha-ha... no need to be so guarded.@k@vS02/11/130700122. I'm not going to bite.@k@vS02/11/130700123. ...Maebara-san.``
Ooishi-san drained the last of his coffee, then stretched luxuriously, making a show of how relaxed he was.
...But that only wound my nerves tighter.
``......Wh-... What do you... want from me this time?``
``Want?@k@vS02/11/130700125. ...Oh, no no, nothing at all.``
He's dodging the question even now...?@k What a thoroughly unreadable man.
``I don't want anything from you, per se.@k@vS02/11/130700127. ...I just thought perhaps you might want something from me, Maebara-san.``
``...I don't... need anything from you.``
``Oh, really...?@k@vS02/11/130700129. Truly?@k@vS02/11/130700130. You're quite sure about that?@k@vS02/11/130700131. Nfu-fu-fu-fu...!``
The rear door of this wagon was only on one side.@k ...Meaning, the only exit was the side Ooishi-san was blocking.
......I was trapped...
.........I could only hang my head limply... and resign myself to silence.
Undeterred by my silence, Ooishi-san leaned back and leisurely began puffing on a cigarette...
...The drone of insects made the seemingly infinite silence stretch even longer.
``......I was so sure you had something you wanted to talk to me about, Maebara-san.``
Ooishi-san exhaled a lazy plume of smoke.
...Even with the window cracked, the purple haze just hung inside the car.
How much... did Ooishi-san actually know?
...What did he already know, and what was he trying to get out of me?
......If knowing the truth got you erased... then if I told him, would Ooishi-san vanish too...?
......Something told me that, in Ooishi-san's case, I didn't need to worry about that.
``......How did things ever come to this, I wonder.``
Ooishi-san murmured it almost to himself, as though thinking aloud—but clearly directing it at me.
...How things came to this...?
How should I know?@k If anything... I was the one who wanted answers.
``...You're acquainted with Jirou Tomitake-san and Miyo Takano-san, aren't you?@k@vS02/11/130700135. I saw you with them during the festival, along with Shion Sonozaki-san—with my own two eyes.``
``Do you happen to know... what's become of Tomitake-san and Takano-san?``
``The truth is... they've passed away.@k@vS02/11/130700138. Most unfortunate deaths, really.``
......Ooishi-san had probably intended that as his trump card—something to shock me.
...But I already knew, so I didn't give him the dramatic reaction he was looking for.
``...Oh?@k@vS02/11/130700140. Perhaps... you already knew?``
...For this particular question, my silence was as good as saying yes.
``Well then... I wonder if you also know this.@k@vS02/11/130700142. ...The reason Tomitake-san and Takano-san fell victim to 'the curse.'``
``......Apparently, they entered some sort of forbidden building that they were never supposed to set foot in.``
......The ritual storehouse.
......That forbidden repository, sealing away Hinamizawa's blood-soaked history.
...The place where it all went wrong...
``Have you heard the rumors... that the village chief and Rika Furude-san were also cursed because of that?``
............Did that mean... it was because of me and Shion...?
...He was right.@k ...The moment Shion and I... opened our mouths about it......
``The ritual storehouse, they call it.@k@vS02/11/130700146. ...Word is that the lock had been replaced with a simpler one, and that's how intruders got in so easily.``
This was the first I'd heard of it.
``Until a couple years ago, the ritual storehouse supposedly had massive bolts and heavy padlocks all over it.``
``But, well—you know how the head priest and his wife passed away, and now little Rika Furude-san is the one looking after the place, right?``
``She apparently asked the village chief to swap them out for a lighter, simpler padlock, because the bolts were too heavy for her to manage.``
.........I didn't know anything about those massive bolts... but the lock had certainly been a simple one.
It was a cheap little padlock—conspicuously flimsy for something guarding a place as important as the ritual storehouse.

``...Rika-san is just a small girl, after all.@k@vS02/11/130700151. Those rust-encrusted bolts must have been terribly hard on her.@k@vS02/11/130700152. ...So the village chief arranged for a craftsman to replace everything with a simple padlock and key.``
``......And... how does that make it bad luck?``

``Here's the thing.@k@vS02/11/130700154. ...With the old, heavy locks, no intruder could have broken in... but the village chief and Rika-san went and replaced them with a cheap lock on their own, and that's how thieves got in—@k@vS02/11/130700155. ...or so the story goes.``
``...That's a bit of a stretch...``

``...And the story that's been making the rounds is that certain villagers took it upon themselves to punish both the thieves who broke into the ritual storehouse and the village chief and Rika-san who changed the lock...@k@vS02/11/130700157. ...or so the whispers go, anyway.``
changeReason: Old opens with 'Letting that slide' which misreads the Japanese about a rumor circulating.

TextENGNew: ``...Maebara-san.@k@vS02/11/130700159. ......What do you make of that?``
``...Wh-What do I make of it? ...I don't even understand who these 'certain villagers' are supposed to be...``
``Are you familiar with the Three Families?@k@vS02/11/130700161. At the Watanagashi opening ceremony, the heads of each family give an address, you know.``
``I didn't see it.@k@vS02/01/130100733. ...I arrived... partway through the festival... so I didn't really...``

``The Three Families are the three oldest lineages in Hinamizawa.@k@vS02/11/130700163. Specifically, we're talking about the Kimiyoshi family, the Sonozaki family, and the Furude family.@k@vS02/11/130700164. ...They're said to go back quite a long way.``
``Since ancient times, all important village decisions were said to be made by the Three Families in council.``
The Kimiyoshi family...... old man Kimiyoshi... in other words, the village chief's family.
The Sonozaki family... was Mion's.
...And the Furude family... was Rika-chan's?

``After the war, the Sonozaki family rapidly expanded their influence.@k@vS02/11/130700167. Once they became the standard-bearers of the anti-dam movement, they effectively rose to the top of Hinamizawa's power structure.``
``But you see, in keeping with the old traditions, even today, important village matters are still decided through consultation among the Three Families.``

``That's why the heads of the Three Families each give an address at the Watanagashi opening ceremony.@k@vS02/11/130700170. ...Namely, the village chief, the old lady who currently leads the Sonozaki family, and the last surviving member of the Furude family—Rika-san.``
``.........I'd heard the Sonozaki family was prestigious... but Rika-chan's family... was that kind of old household too?``

``...Well, as you know, she has no surviving relatives.@k@vS02/11/130700172. In practice, the Furude family holds virtually no influence.@k@vS02/11/130700173. However, Rika-san herself seems to have a certain magnetic pull—I hear many of the village elders are absolutely devoted to her.``
......It was certainly true that Rika-chan had a peculiar charisma among the older villagers.
``...So... what's the point of telling me all this about the Three Families?``
At that, Ooishi-san paused dramatically and lit a fresh cigarette.
``Even after all that explanation, you still need me to spell it out...?``
``...I'm asking because I don't follow.``
Ooishi-san gave a meaningful, sardonic smile and exhaled a long stream of smoke.

``......Members of the Three Families are being eliminated, one after another.@k@vS02/11/130700176. That's what I'm getting at.@k@vS02/11/130700177. ...My theory is that it's rooted in some old custom or tradition within Hinamizawa, but beyond that, I'm completely in the dark.``
``I'm just as much in the dark as you are.``
``That's precisely why I'm asking!@k@vS02/11/130700179. ...The heads of the Kimiyoshi and Furude families have been taken out—so wouldn't the Sonozaki family's head be next?``
``...Well, that's just a notion that may or may not have crossed my mind lately...... Na-ha-ha-ha!@k@vS02/11/130700181. Just kidding, just kidding...``
``Th-The head of the Sonozaki family... Wh-Who is that...?!``
``The official head is the old lady at the main house, but it would be fair to say that virtually all real authority has already passed to her granddaughter—Mion-san.``
The image of Mion flashed through my mind—briskly directing the adults during the search for Rika-chan...
``M-Mion...?!@k@vS02/01/130100738_2. She's going to disappear?!``
...A possibility I'd never even considered... every hair on my body stood on end.@k ...That's insane!!
``Not a pleasant thought, is it?``
``O-Of course not...!!!@k@vS02/01/130100740. That's... absolutely unacceptable!!!``
Firmly.@k ...Ooishi-san seized both my shoulders and pulled me close.
``Then cooperate with us, Maebara-san.``
``......Tell me anything you've noticed about Mion Sonozaki-san lately. Anything at all.``
changeReason: New adds the honorific '-san' to Sonozaki's name ('Mion Sonozaki-san'), reflecting Japanese politeness conventions in the context of a police officer speaking.

TextENGNew: ...Had I been manipulated...?
By the time I realized it, it was already too late.
Ooishi-san leaned in so close our noses nearly touched... waiting for my next words.
...The pressure was overwhelming...@k I couldn't tear my eyes away... from that gaze...
...Just then, a beeping sound rang out.@k ...From the driver's seat.
He ignored it at first, but when it kept going insistently, Ooishi-san finally released me and leaned forward into the driver's seat.
``......Hello?@k@vS02/11/130700189. Yes, yes—loud and clear.``
Saved...
Not that I could actually use the opening to escape, mind you...
...It sounded like he was being ordered to return.
...The moment I realized I was going to be let go, all the tension drained from my body.
``It seems I need to head back.@k@vS02/11/130700196. Hmm, and we were just getting to the good part... What a shame.``
Ooishi-san slid the door open, stepped outside, then circled around to the driver's seat.
The door was left wide open.@k ......Did that mean I was free to go...?

``You're a fine young man, Maebara-san—truly devoted to your friends. Quite admirable for someone your age.@k@vS02/11/130700198. I completely understand that talking to the police about your friends feels like a betrayal.``

``Maebara-san.@k@vS02/11/130700200. I'll give you the night. Get some rest and think it over carefully.@k@vS02/11/130700201. ...Just a small amount of courage on your part... might be what saves a great many of your friends.``
......Was he saying that the victims wouldn't stop at Rika-chan and Satoko?

``I'm sure you're aware, but every night since Watanagashi, something has happened.@k@vS02/11/130700203. ...In past years, the curse came once a year—but this year, it's been one night after another.@k@vS02/11/130700204. Quite the bountiful year, wouldn't you say.``
``...There's nothing 'bountiful' about this.``

``Two nights ago, last night, and now tonight.@k@vS02/11/130700206. There is absolutely no guarantee that nothing will happen tomorrow night.@k@vS02/11/130700207. Preventing these things before they happen is our job—but for that, your cooperation is essential, Maebara-san.``
I peered out through the open door... and found that we were parked directly in front of my own house.
...It unsettled me that this man knew my name and my address... but right now I was too exhausted to care.@k ...I was too exhausted to care about anything.
``......Well, you must be tired by now.@k@vS02/11/130700209. It's nearly three o'clock.@k@vS02/11/130700210. Please, get a good night's rest.``
As I moved to climb out of the wagon, Ooishi-san spoke up behind me—his voice suddenly sharp, like a parting shot.
``I'll be coming by every day until you're ready to talk, you know.@k@vS02/11/130700212. ...Forcing confessions out of people in interrogation rooms—that's just not how we do things anymore.``
I had no words to fire back, but slamming the door shut behind me served as answer enough.
After a light tap of the horn, Ooishi-san's wagon pulled away into the night...
Without watching it go, I turned toward the front door.
......The door was locked, but the chain wasn't latched.
I turned the key and stepped inside...
...Then headed for the stairs.
............That was where consciousness left me.
